title: Simple Simpson

jokes.text: On FOX, the home of promiscuous idiots.
jokes.setup: Introducing a reality show called 'Promiscuous Idiots Island'
jokes.punchline: FOX calling itself 'the home of promiscuous idiots'
jokes.explanation: Sharp meta-commentary on FOX's programming, well-structured and economical

jokes.text: Get ready, skanks. Here comes the truth train!
jokes.setup: Host preparing to reveal a 'shocking secret'
jokes.punchline: The ridiculously aggressive tone combined with 'truth train' metaphor
jokes.explanation: Funny phrasing but fairly standard reality TV parody

jokes.text: This isn't an island at all. It's a peninsula.
jokes.setup: Building up to a shocking revelation about deception
jokes.punchline: The 'shocking' secret is a trivial geographic technicality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - subverts expectation of meaningful deception with absurd technicality

jokes.text: Well, you don't need a boat cause you can walk!
jokes.setup: Woman wants to take a boat to leave
jokes.punchline: Revealing the practical implication of it being a peninsula
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the peninsula joke, logically follows through the premise

jokes.text: When you sign a contract with FOX, you know you're going to be betrayed and humiliated.
jokes.setup: Marge commenting on the reality show deception
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact statement about FOX's business model
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer observation - casual acceptance of network cynicism with good character voice

jokes.text: If we don't watch these, it's like we're stealing tv.
jokes.setup: Marge telling Homer to be quiet during commercials
jokes.punchline: Homer's backwards logic about watching commercials
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic - completely wrong but delivered with such earnestness it becomes endearing

jokes.text: The five-story pig shredder, blessed by three popes.
jokes.setup: Tour guide describing gruesome meat processing equipment
jokes.punchline: Adding religious blessing to industrial horror
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of industrial brutality with religious sanctification

jokes.text: If you don't like bacon, well, then the hell with you.
jokes.setup: Enthusiastic bacon promotion
jokes.punchline: Sudden hostility toward non-bacon lovers
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the extremist bacon cult mentality

jokes.text: The only thing that can make bacon more delicious is seeing how it's made!
jokes.setup: Just heard disturbing details about meat processing
jokes.punchline: Homer's enthusiasm is completely undeterred by the horror
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - his love of food overcomes any moral qualms about production methods

jokes.text: Mr. Simpson, if you really want to kill yourself, I also sell handguns.
jokes.setup: Homer frantically buying bacon products
jokes.punchline: Apu's casual offer of a more direct suicide method
jokes.explanation: Dark but efficient - Apu's matter-of-fact business approach to Homer's self-destruction

jokes.text: But I'm no super-genius. Or are I?
jokes.setup: Homer doubting his ability to judge pigs
jokes.punchline: Grammar error while questioning his intelligence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - the grammar error while questioning intelligence is pitch-perfect character comedy

jokes.text: Hey, I know that country!
jokes.setup: Performer says he's going to sing about America
jokes.punchline: Homer's excited recognition of his own country
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - excited about the most obvious things as if they're discoveries

jokes.text: if you don't buy my record... then Al-Qaeda wins!
jokes.setup: Patriotic country song
jokes.punchline: Exploiting post-9/11 fears for record sales
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of post-9/11 political manipulation and commercialization of fear

jokes.text: Clearly had a snout job.
jokes.setup: Homer judging pigs in competition
jokes.punchline: Pig plastic surgery pun on 'nose job'
jokes.explanation: Solid pun that works in context of pig judging

jokes.text: Too '80s.
jokes.setup: Homer judging pigs
jokes.punchline: Applying fashion criticism to livestock
jokes.explanation: Absurd application of style criticism to pigs

jokes.text: How is someone so lame related to a hot tamale like me?
jokes.setup: Bart seeing Lisa's place-setting competition
jokes.punchline: Bart's self-aggrandizing comparison to his sister
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart cockiness but nothing exceptional

jokes.text: Dribble glass, rubber knife, whoopee salad...
jokes.setup: Describing place setting
jokes.punchline: All items are joke/prank versions
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's prankster nature applied to formal dining

jokes.text: The setting features tuning forks, champagne flutes and of course, chopsticks.
jokes.setup: Lisa's musical-themed place setting
jokes.punchline: All items are musical instruments that happen to be dining implements
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Lisa's intelligence and creativity

jokes.text: Ella Fitz Jell-O
jokes.setup: Lisa describing her dessert
jokes.punchline: Ella Fitzgerald pun with Jell-O
jokes.explanation: Sophisticated pun that shows Lisa's cultural knowledge

jokes.text: Lordy, girl, your entry stinks like the south end of a north-bound mule!
jokes.setup: Judge critiquing Lisa's place setting
jokes.punchline: Colorful southern metaphor for something smelling bad
jokes.explanation: Standard folksy insult, not particularly original

jokes.text: Your place-setting thinks it's better than it is, like a yard dog that sneaks into the house!
jokes.setup: Continuing harsh critique of Lisa
jokes.punchline: Another folksy metaphor about pretension
jokes.explanation: Another folksy metaphor but getting repetitive

jokes.text: Well, you did put it on a table.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking if she did anything right
jokes.punchline: The most basic possible accomplishment
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of minimal praise

jokes.text: Wobble, wobble, wobble!
jokes.setup: Judge pointing out table instability
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic demonstration of the wobbling
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy but not particularly clever

jokes.text: This table's about as solid as your underlying concept.
jokes.setup: Criticizing the wobbly table
jokes.punchline: Connecting physical instability to conceptual weakness
jokes.explanation: Good metaphorical connection between physical and abstract failings

jokes.text: I didn't know I could fit in a bubble. I must be losin' weight.
jokes.setup: Homer trapped in police bubble
jokes.punchline: Homer's positive spin on being contained
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - finding the silver lining in police containment

jokes.text: We all know pi-r-squared. But today, pie are justice.
jokes.setup: Homer in costume delivering superhero line
jokes.punchline: Mathematical formula pun with pie/pi
jokes.explanation: Clever mathematical pun that works as both superhero catchphrase and Homer-level wordplay

jokes.text: Wherever people are mistreated, the pie man will strike!
jokes.setup: Homer establishing his superhero mission
jokes.punchline: Grandiose declaration with food-based weaponry
jokes.explanation: Standard superhero parody but fits Homer's character well

jokes.text: Gee, I thought I had a bigger package than that.
jokes.setup: Lisa showing drawing of Pie Man
jokes.punchline: Homer's innocent comment with sexual undertones
jokes.explanation: Standard double entendre, Homer's innocent delivery makes it work

jokes.text: Maybe you're right, Lisa. Maybe you're pie. Pie-be you're pie.
jokes.setup: Homer agreeing with Lisa about not using pies for vigilantism
jokes.punchline: Gradually replacing words with 'pie' until it's nonsensical
jokes.explanation: Escalating wordplay that shows Homer's pie obsession overriding logic

jokes.text: It's 'fantastic floor'! My family can't put in floors we rent!
jokes.setup: Customer complaining about wrong comic
jokes.punchline: Absurd complaint about home improvement comic being useless to renters
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist complaint combining superhero parody with mundane concerns

jokes.text: Don't do the crime if you can't do the key lime.
jokes.setup: Pie Man confronting Comic Book Guy
jokes.punchline: Classic saying twisted with pie reference
jokes.explanation: Solid pun on classic phrase that fits the pie theme well

jokes.text: There is no way I'm going to be dating a man with pie on his face.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy's romantic delusions
jokes.punchline: Celebrity's practical concerns about pie-covered face
jokes.explanation: Good practical response to the absurd situation

jokes.text: What about fistface? ... I demand you reveal the identity of fistface! It's you!
jokes.setup: Kids discussing superheroes
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's violent superhero alter-ego revealed
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Milhouse's secret tough-guy fantasy

jokes.text: Oh, I think you'd find his filling extremely hot.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Pie Man's attractiveness
jokes.punchline: Sexual double entendre using pie terminology
jokes.explanation: Typical Moe sleazy comment, fits character but relies on obvious sexual innuendo

jokes.text: Flanders!? He's not man enough to trim my crust. I mean pie man's crust. I'm not pie man.
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting Ned might be Pie Man
jokes.punchline: Homer's indignant denial accidentally revealing himself
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer slip-up revealing his secret identity through ego

jokes.text: Pie-- popular pastry, tricky math thing, and now sword of righteousness.
jokes.setup: News report about Pie Man
jokes.punchline: Three definitions of 'pie' escalating to heroic metaphor
jokes.explanation: Clever escalating definitions building to heroic metaphor

jokes.text: This is for your show's slight decline in quality over the years.
jokes.setup: Pie Man attacking Kent Brockman
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on TV show quality decline
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary that could apply to any long-running show including The Simpsons itself

jokes.text: Oh, those poor innocent pies.
jokes.setup: Hearing about pies being destroyed
jokes.punchline: Homer's genuine concern for the pastries
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - more concerned about food than weapons or violence

jokes.text: Search me. Come back when you want a giant rack.
jokes.setup: Sick child asking about medical treatment
jokes.punchline: Doctor only cares about cosmetic surgery profits
jokes.explanation: Dark satire of medical priorities and cosmetic surgery culture

jokes.text: No one ever wants to hear about the importance of pants clips, do they?
jokes.setup: Wiggum explaining why he dislikes Pie Man
jokes.punchline: Focusing on the most mundane aspect of bike safety
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum - obsessed with trivial safety details while missing bigger picture

jokes.text: Homer Simpson away. I mean, the pie man.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to escape while maintaining secret identity
jokes.punchline: Accidentally using his real name
jokes.explanation: Classic superhero secret identity fumble, perfectly Homer

jokes.text: That's positive, calling me a jerk? You are a jerk.
jokes.setup: Wiggum trying to give positive reinforcement
jokes.punchline: Reinforcement devolving into more insults
jokes.explanation: Good police incompetence moment showing their dysfunctional dynamic

jokes.text: I'm looking up your nose, but I feel like I'm staring into your soul.
jokes.setup: Marge tending to wounded Homer
jokes.punchline: Romantic intimacy from awkward medical angle
jokes.explanation: Beautiful Marge moment - finding romance in the most unromantic circumstances

jokes.text: That was so exciting, yet familiar. Like coke with lemon.
jokes.setup: Marge reflecting on the rescue experience
jokes.punchline: Comparing adventure to minor beverage variation
jokes.explanation: Good Marge observation - mundane comparison for extraordinary experience

jokes.text: I... murdered your father.
jokes.setup: Lisa recognizing Homer as Pie Man
jokes.punchline: Homer's dramatic but completely untrue confession
jokes.explanation: Good superhero secret identity parody with Homer's typical dramatic flair

jokes.text: We've been getting his mail for weeks.
jokes.setup: Homer denying he's Pie Man
jokes.punchline: Practical evidence that ruins the secret identity
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of secret identity tropes with mundane reality

jokes.text: Dad, this is the basement.
jokes.setup: Homer calling it his 'secret lair'
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Good reality check on Homer's superhero pretensions

jokes.text: Welcome to my secret lair. Dad, this is the basement.
jokes.setup: Homer dramatically revealing secret hideout
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of superhero pretensions with Lisa's practical observation

jokes.text: You're not mild-mannered. You're often liquored-up and rude.
jokes.setup: Homer describing himself as 'mild-mannered'
jokes.punchline: Lisa's accurate character assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa - brutally honest assessment of Homer's actual personality

jokes.text: I was in so deep, I forgot pies were food.
jokes.setup: Homer deciding what to do with his remaining pies
jokes.punchline: Admission that he lost sight of pies' primary purpose
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer moment - losing track of food's basic purpose is peak character comedy

jokes.text: Aw, we wish you could eat us, too.
jokes.setup: Homer expressing desire to eat all the pies
jokes.punchline: The pies reciprocating the cannibalistic sentiment
jokes.explanation: Dark but sweet absurdist moment about mutual consumption desires

jokes.text: I've run out of pie-related puns.
jokes.setup: Pie Man confronting Mr. Burns
jokes.punchline: Breaking character to acknowledge the writing challenge
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about the constraints of themed superhero dialogue

jokes.text: It's not a rubber band; it's a scrunchie!
jokes.setup: Burns describing Homer's costume construction
jokes.punchline: Homer's indignant correction about fashion accessory
jokes.explanation: Good Homer vanity moment - caring about fashion details in costume critique

jokes.text: No, not community service! I can't stand helping others! I just can't!
jokes.setup: Burns threatening to reveal Homer's identity
jokes.punchline: Homer more terrified of helping people than jail time
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - finding altruism more horrifying than punishment

jokes.text: like every monster, he just wants to be loved.
jokes.setup: Homer's target revealed to be Ned Flanders
jokes.punchline: Calling the kindest neighbor a 'monster' while defending him
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - simultaneously insulting and defending Flanders

jokes.text: Grow a spine on your own time, blimpo.
jokes.setup: Homer hesitating to attack a child
jokes.punchline: Burns' callous dismissal of conscience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns - combining cruelty with workplace efficiency language

jokes.text: All his talk of peace and love is really honking off my red chinese masters.
jokes.setup: Burns explaining why Dalai Lama must be attacked
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of foreign political allegiances
jokes.explanation: Good Burns villainy - casually admitting international conspiracy

jokes.text: Please forgive me, talking llama.
jokes.setup: Homer about to attack the Dalai Lama
jokes.punchline: Confusing Dalai Lama with Dalai Llama
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer malapropism at the worst possible moment

jokes.text: His brain isn't large enough to juggle two contrasting personae!
jokes.setup: Continued disbelief about Homer being Pie Man
jokes.punchline: Questioning Homer's mental capacity for dual identity
jokes.explanation: Smart joke using psychological terminology to describe Homer's limitations

jokes.text: Yeah, and Homer's a dumbass! No offense, Homer. Ya dumbass.
jokes.setup: Adding to criticism of Homer's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting his 'no offense' disclaimer
jokes.explanation: Good Barney moment showing his crude social skills

jokes.text: The pie man could fly. And spit acid! And animals did his bidding!
jokes.setup: Crowd defending why Homer can't be Pie Man
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd superhero powers attributed to Pie Man
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of mythologized superhero abilities

jokes.text: Dad, I think you created a hero that even you couldn't live up to.
jokes.setup: Crowd's disbelief about Homer's heroic capabilities
jokes.punchline: Lisa's insightful analysis of Homer's situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Lisa's wisdom about identity and aspiration

jokes.text: You'd have to be an idiot not to see it from the start.
jokes.setup: Marge saying she always knew Homer was Pie Man
jokes.punchline: Inadvertently insulting everyone who didn't figure it out
jokes.explanation: Good Marge moment - unintentionally insulting while being supportive

jokes.text: And wherever pie man is, the cupcake kid will not be far behind.
jokes.setup: Homer declaring his continued vigilantism
jokes.punchline: Bart establishing his own food-based sidekick identity
jokes.explanation: Nice family bonding moment extending the superhero theme

jokes.text: While you two are up on the roof, why don't you take the leaves out of the gutter?
jokes.setup: Homer and Bart playing superheroes on roof
jokes.punchline: Marge turning fantasy into household chores
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge - practical mom deflating superhero fantasy with domestic reality

tmdb_overview: Homer becomes "Pie Man", a masked vigilante who delivers a pie in the face of justice to evildoers. However, after attacking Mr. Burns, Burns hires him as a personal hitman, until Homer is ordered to attack the Dalai Lama.
